name: Pralines 2
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-size heavy saucepan, combine the sugar, light brown sugar, water, evaporated milk, butter, and salt.
2. Bring mixture to a boil over medium heat.
3. Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring constantly, until a soft ball forms when a small amount of the mixture is dropped into cold water.
4. Add pecans and cook for an additional 4 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract for 1 minute.
6. Drop about two tablespoonfuls at a time onto a buttered platter or waxed paper.
7. Let pralines harden, then store in a tightly covered container in a cool place.
Makes about 40 pralines.
Lagniappe: Pralines will keep for up to one week without any loss of texture or flavor. Do not freeze. An excellent candy or dessert. Each praline contains approximately 198 calories.
